title: Radio Button UI Component in Design Systems
content: # Radio button Systems Directories Components Articles Blueprints Guides Glossary Biweekly Digest Created by Accordion Action bar Action button Action list Alert banner App bar Autocomplete Avatar Badges Bottom sheet Breadcrumbs Button Button group Cards Checkbox Chip Code snippet Combobox Data table Date picker Dialog Divider Drawer Dropdown Empty state Filter input Form Help text Icon Inline message Label Link List Menus Modal Navigation bar Page header Pagination Popover Progress bar Progress indicator Search Segmented control Select Sidebar Slider Snackbar Switch Tabs Tag Text area Text field Tooltip Tree view Keep up with the latest in Design Systems Subscribe Radio button is a visual control element used to allow users to make a single selection from a predefined set of mutually exclusive options. It is typically represented as a small circle, which is filled or highlighted when selected. Radio buttons are used in scenarios where all available options must be visible and a user is required to select exactly one. Radio buttons are often grouped in radio button groups to clarify that the choices are mutually exclusive. By providing a clear, easy way for users to make single selections, the Radio button component contributes significantly to a streamlined, intuitive user experience.
